title: Live in Canada permanently - Canada.ca
content:
## Live in Canada permanently
Explore permanent residence programs to move to Canada, such as Express Entry, family sponsorship and regional programs.
Foreign nationals living in the Democratic Republic of the Congo, South Sudan and Uganda can’t travel to Canada as of May 27, 2026.
Find out more about these measures
Skilled workers, tradespeople

### Provincial Nominee Program (PNP)

### Rural and Francophone Community Immigration Pilots (RCIP, FCIP)

### Atlantic Immigration Program (AIP)

### Quebec-selected skilled workers

### Quebec investors and entrepreneurs

### Sponsor your spouse or family members

### Hong Kong residents in Canada

### Foreign nationals who were in state care

## Looking for something else?
Work in Canada for a few years with a work permit

### Explore all immigration programs
Answer a few questions to get a list of programs to explore

## Programs that are paused or closed

### Start-up Visa Paused

### Self-employed persons Paused

### Economic Mobility Pathways Pilot (EMPP) Closed

### Temporary resident (TR) to permanent resident (PR) pathway Closed

### Agri-Food Pilot Closed

### People affected by the conflict in Sudan Closed

### Family-based program for Colombians, Haitians and Venezuelans Closed

### Rural and Northern Immigration Pilot (RNIP) Closed

### Out-of-status construction workers Closed

### Ukrainian nationals with family in Canada Closed

### Families of flight PS752 victims Closed

### Resettled Yazidis and survivors of Daesh Closed

### Francophone immigration
Live, work or study in French in a Francophone community outside Quebec
- Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada
- Immigration and Refugee Board of Canada
